# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  const { method, headers, body } = request;

  if (method !== 'POST') {
    return new Response('Method Not Allowed', { status: 405 });
  }

  // Validate and sanitize input
  const contentType = headers.get('content-type');
  if (!contentType || !contentType.startsWith('application/json')) {
    return new Response('Invalid Content-Type', { status: 415 });
  }

  try {
    const data = await body.json();
    const userContent = data.content;
    const userHeaders = data.headers;

    // Validate user content and headers
    if (typeof userContent !== 'string' || typeof userHeaders !== 'object') {
      return new Response('Invalid request payload', { status: 400 });
    }

    // Create a ReadableStream from the user content
    const readableStream = new ReadableStream({
      start(controller) {
        controller.enqueue(userContent);
        controller.close();
      },
    });

    // Set response headers
    const responseHeaders = new Headers(userHeaders);

    // Return the streamed response
    return new Response(readableStream, { status: 200, headers: responseHeaders });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error processing request:', error);
    return new Response('Internal Server Error', { status: 500 });
  }
}
